title: Windows PowerShell 5.1 syntax
text: When a repo targets Windows PowerShell 5.1, write syntax that parses under powershell.exe Desktop. Prefer explicit if/else, loops, functions, and compatible .NET APIs.
warnings: Validate with powershell.exe, not pwsh.
Do not assume newer parser behavior.

title: PowerShell 7-only syntax to avoid
text: Avoid ternary, null-coalescing (??), null-conditional assignment (??=), null-conditional access (?. / ?[]), pipeline chains (&& / ||), ForEach-Object -Parallel, Join-String, and backtick-e / backtick-u escapes in Windows PowerShell 5.1 scripts. Each has a documented 5.1-safe alternative.
warnings: If the task explicitly targets pwsh, say so and validate with pwsh.
The 5.1 doc views do not document these operators; they are PowerShell 6/7 additions.

title: #Requires -Version
text: Declare #Requires -Version 5.1 at the top of scripts that depend on the Windows PowerShell engine. PowerShell enforces #Requires before running the script and refuses to run when prerequisites are not met.
warnings: #Requires must be the first item on its own line.
-RunAsAdministrator is also available when elevation is mandatory.

title: Grouping resets $? in 5.1
text: Until PowerShell 7, wrapping a command in (...), $(...), or @(...) resets $? to True even when the inner command failed. Capture $? or $LASTEXITCODE before grouping.
warnings: Do not read $? after a grouped expression in Windows PowerShell 5.1.
Decide native success from $LASTEXITCODE.

title: Native command quoting
text: Invoke quoted native executable paths with the call operator and pass arguments separately. Use argument arrays for complex calls.
warnings: Do not wrap an entire command line as one string.
Do not expect cmd.exe splitting rules in PowerShell.

title: Call operator ampersand
text: Use & '<TOOLS_ROOT>\tool.exe' @argv when the executable path is quoted or stored in a variable. The call operator runs a command whose name is in a string; it never parses a command line into command plus arguments.
warnings: Without &, a quoted path is just a string expression.
& 'tool.exe --flag' fails; keep the path and arguments separate.

title: Argument arrays
text: Build native command arguments as arrays such as @('--input', '<WORK_ROOT>\file with spaces.txt') so each element is passed as one argument.
warnings: Avoid nested quote construction unless the target tool requires literal quotes.
Log the final argument list when debugging.

title: Stop-parsing token
text: Place --% before native arguments to pass the rest of the line literally except %NAME% environment expansion. Useful when a tool needs characters PowerShell would interpret.
warnings: Windows native commands only; effective until end of line or pipe.
No stream redirection and no $variables after --%.

title: LASTEXITCODE
text: Check $LASTEXITCODE immediately after native executables. $? is PowerShell pipeline status; for native commands it is True only when $LASTEXITCODE is 0.
warnings: Do not claim success when a native command returned nonzero.
Store the exit code before another native command runs.

title: stderr handling
text: Native stderr may contain warnings, banners, or progress. Capture and log stdout/stderr intentionally, then judge failure by exit code and tool contract.
warnings: Do not treat all stderr as failure.
Do not ignore stderr when diagnosing failures.

title: NativeCommandError under ErrorActionPreference Stop
text: Practical tip: with $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop', merging a native command's stderr via 2>&1 can wrap stderr lines in NativeCommandError records and make a successful command look failed. Redirect stderr to a file instead and decide by exit code.
warnings: This is a field-tested rule about native-command behavior under ErrorActionPreference Stop.
Always verify against the tool's documented exit-code contract.

title: Phase-marker logging pattern
text: Use a small Write-Phase helper that timestamps a message, writes it to the console, and appends it to a task log with explicit encoding. Log START, phase markers, paths, command lines, exit codes, and one final deterministic status line.
warnings: End every task script with exactly one deterministic status (VALIDATED, FAILED_WITH_EVIDENCE, SKIPPED_BY_SCOPE, BLOCKED_WITH_EVIDENCE, NOT_ATTEMPTED).
Never use vague confidence wording in statuses.

title: Common tool exit-code contracts
text: Documented exit contracts: python exits 1 on uncaught exception and 2 on usage error; python -m py_compile exits nonzero on syntax errors; cargo fails with status 101; npm run propagates the script's exit code (scripts run via cmd.exe on Windows).
warnings: Judge tools by their documented contracts, not stderr volume.
Record the contract source when relying on a nonstandard code.

title: try/catch catches terminating errors only
text: try/catch handles terminating errors only; most cmdlet errors are non-terminating by default. Escalate with -ErrorAction Stop per command or $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop' script-wide. Inside catch, $_ is an ErrorRecord; finally always runs.
warnings: Catch specific exception types before generic catch blocks.
-ErrorAction overrides the preference variable for that one command.

title: Visual Studio Developer Shell
text: Load the Visual Studio developer environment before MSVC/MSBuild work and verify VSCMD_VER, architecture variables, tool paths, INCLUDE, LIB, and PATH.
warnings: Do not assume Build Tools path is fixed.
Loading is evidence to verify, not a state to assume.

title: VsDevCmd.bat
text: Use VsDevCmd.bat through cmd.exe when command-prompt semantics are needed. Child cmd environment changes do not update the parent PowerShell session automatically.
warnings: Use Launch-VsDevShell.ps1 when the current PowerShell process needs environment updates.
Do not mix Visual Studio versions or switch environments inside one window.

title: Launch-VsDevShell.ps1
text: Launch-VsDevShell.ps1 (in <VSINSTALLDIR>\Common7\Tools) loads the DevShell module and invokes Enter-VsDevShell, updating the current PowerShell process. Use -Arch and -HostArch (VS2022 17.1+) and -SkipAutomaticLocation for automation.
warnings: Exact VSINSTALLDIR varies by version, edition, and machine.
Setting only -Arch makes the shell try to match the host; set both for cross builds.

title: Detect initialized developer shell via VSCMD_VER
text: The developer shells set VSCMD_VER (since VS2015). The recommended way to detect whether a developer environment is already initialized in the console is to check whether VSCMD_VER is defined.
warnings: Re-loading a different environment into an initialized shell is discouraged.
Record the detected VSCMD_VER in the task log.

title: vswhere discovery without PATH assumptions
text: vswhere.exe is installed at %ProgramFiles(x86)%\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vswhere.exe with VS 2017+ and is not on PATH by default. It lists instances (for example -legacy -prerelease -format json) and supports filtering; Build Tools components are identified by IDs such as Microsoft.Component.MSBuild and the Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.VCTools workload.
warnings: Do not assume vswhere is on PATH.
Prefer repo-provided resolved paths over discovery when available.

title: Multiple Visual Studio instances
text: Multiple Visual Studio versions and editions can coexist on one machine. Choose one instance explicitly, record which instance was used, and never run command files from different versions in the same shell.
warnings: PATH order is not a selection mechanism.
Pick the instance whose toolset the repo requires.

title: MSBuild
text: Invoke MSBuild with an explicit project or solution, -m for parallel (default is 1 process without it), -t: targets, -p:Configuration and -p:Platform, optional -restore and -bl binary log, then check LASTEXITCODE.
warnings: Do not rely on partial output files when MSBuild failed.
Record the selected MSBuild path and arguments.

title: MSBuild switch lists from PowerShell
text: Every MSBuild switch has -switch and /switch forms and is case-insensitive. When invoking MSBuild from PowerShell instead of cmd.exe, semicolon- or comma-separated lists passed to a switch may need quoting so the shell does not split them.
warnings: Build arguments as array elements; quote elements containing spaces or separators.
Verify the parsed command in the build log when in doubt.

title: Developer Shell setup placement
text: Developer Shell setup belongs at the top of a build script, executed once and verified, before any compile or MSBuild step. The recommended approach is to start a fresh shell per environment rather than switching environments mid-session.
warnings: Verify VSCMD_ARG_TGT_ARCH matches the intended target before building.
Fix the environment instead of hardcoding include or lib paths.

title: MSVC
text: Verify cl.exe, link.exe, INCLUDE, LIB, PATH, WindowsSdkDir, and VCToolsInstallDir before diagnosing compiler failures.
warnings: Missing headers or libraries usually indicate an environment problem.
Do not guess hardcoded include paths before verifying dev shell setup.

title: Embedded Python
text: When generating Python from PowerShell, use single-quoted line arrays (verbatim strings prevent accidental interpolation), write under <WORK_ROOT>, and validate with python -m py_compile before running.
warnings: Avoid accidental PowerShell interpolation of Python variables.
Double single quotes to embed them inside a single-quoted line.

title: Injecting values into generated Python
text: Pass values into generated Python via command-line arguments (sys.argv), a JSON input file, or environment variables, never by interpolating values into Python source. This keeps the source stable and validation meaningful.
warnings: If interpolation is unavoidable, use -f format strings into single-quoted templates and validate immediately.
Python readers of PowerShell-written JSON should use encoding utf-8-sig.

title: Encoding and BOM
text: Windows PowerShell 5.1 Set-Content -Encoding UTF8 writes UTF-8 with BOM, and 5.1 defaults differ from PowerShell 7. Always pass -Encoding explicitly; use .NET UTF8Encoding(false) when a consumer requires BOM-less UTF-8.
warnings: Never rely on default encodings across engines.
Use utf-8-sig in Python readers when BOM may be present (practical tip).

title: Long path caution
text: Practical tip: Windows fully qualified paths are historically limited to about 260 characters; deep output trees fail with misleading errors. Keep artifact roots short and shorten the root rather than relying on backslash-question prefixes that many tools do not handle uniformly.
warnings: Verify long-path support in the consuming tool before relying on it.
Watch for failures that only reproduce on long paths.

title: Put $null on the left of comparisons
text: Comparison operators filter arrays instead of returning a Boolean when the left operand is an array. Write if ($null -eq $value) so null checks behave correctly even when the value is a collection.
warnings: if ($value -eq $null) silently misbehaves for arrays.
Containment and type operators always return Booleans.

title: -join and -split operators
text: Windows PowerShell 5.1 joins with the -join operator ($items -join ', ') and splits with -split, whose delimiter is a regex by default. The Join-String cmdlet is PowerShell 7 only.
warnings: Unary -join binds tighter than the comma: parenthesize -join ('a','b').
Escape regex metacharacters in -split delimiters or use the SimpleMatch option.

title: AST validation
text: Use [System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile to catch PowerShell syntax errors before running changed scripts, and run the validator under powershell.exe when 5.1 is the target.
warnings: A clean parse under pwsh proves nothing about 5.1.
List every parse error with its line number.

title: Smoke checks after parsing
text: After a clean AST parse, run the cheapest real execution the script supports: -Help or usage mode, -WhatIf or dry-run, or a minimal no-op invocation, asserting exit code and expected output.
warnings: Parse plus smoke catches most generation mistakes before expensive runs.
Record smoke-check commands and exit codes.

title: JSON and JSONL validation
text: Parse generated JSON with ConvertFrom-Json and parse JSONL line by line, reporting the first invalid line number. When metadata files cross-reference each other, validate that every referenced file exists and every referenced id is defined.
warnings: File existence is not sufficient validation.
Validate source id cross-references when metadata files refer to each other.

title: Artifact routing
text: Put prompt archives under <PROMPTS_ROOT>, work scripts and logs under <WORK_ROOT>, and reports under <REPORTS_ROOT>. Keep task artifacts out of .agents\skills.
warnings: Skill folders should contain reusable skill content only.
Do not place temp validation scripts inside the skill folder.

title: Detecting repo conventions
text: Detect a repo's conventions from its agent instruction files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md), contributor docs, .vscode/tasks.json (shell vs process task types), and build manifests (solution files, Cargo.toml, package.json, pyproject.toml). Map the discovered roots onto the placeholder names before doing work.
warnings: Repo-local instructions always win over generic guidance.
If no convention exists, propose one instead of scattering artifacts.

title: Maintaining skill metadata
text: When guidance materially changes, update topics.json (warnings, applies_to) and add matching retrieval chunks to corpus.jsonl. Keep the metadata and the topic files in sync.
warnings: Label field-tested rules as practical tips, not authoritative fact.
Keep skill docs portable: no single project's internals.

title: No Git unless requested
text: Run Git commands only when the user or task explicitly asks for Git validation. If skipped, state that Git validation was skipped by user preference.
warnings: Do not claim Git cleanliness without running authorized Git validation.
No git status, diff, branch, log, or remote commands when prohibited.
